title: Umami Crispy Australian Yellow Pepper Bowl
description: A bright, flexitarian bowl that marries crispy tofu and sweet yellow pepper with a briny umami glaze, served in a traditional ttukbaegi for a comforting Aussie twist.
instructions: Heat a dry ttukbaegi over medium heat until hot, then add 1 tsp sesame oil and swirl to coat.
Add cubed tofu and sauté until all sides are golden and crisp, about 8 minutes, then set aside.
In the same pot, add remaining 1 tsp sesame oil and toss in sliced yellow pepper and diced water chestnuts; cook for 5 minutes until pepper soft but still crisp.
While vegetables cook, whisk soy sauce, miso paste, rice vinegar, minced garlic, grated ginger, and chili flakes in a small bowl to make the umami glaze.
Pour the glaze over the pepper and chestnuts, stir, and simmer until sauce thickens, about 3 minutes.
Return the crispy tofu to the pot, toss gently to coat with glaze, and heat through for 2 minutes.
Transfer the mixture to a serving bowl, sprinkle sliced scallions and optional toasted sesame seeds on top.
Serve hot straight from the ttukbaegi, pairing with a side of steamed jasmine rice or quinoa if desired.
